title: Sourcegraph/MCP code-intelligence — synthesize a definition site and its callers across two repos
goal: Plan a cross-repo refactor using BOTH the definition location and the caller set found in earlier sessions.

steps.user_request: Use the Sourcegraph MCP to find where build_memory_system is defined and record the repo and path.

expected_memory_writes.def-site: build_memory_system is defined in repo memory-bench at membench/memory_systems/__init__.py (the factory).

steps.user_request: Use find-references to locate callers of build_memory_system in the SEPARATE harbor-runner repo and record them.

expected_memory_writes.caller-set: build_memory_system callers in repo harbor-runner: runner/launch.py and runner/replay.py (cross-repo callers, scope key = repo:harbor-runner).

memory_probes.description: Cross-repo callers persisted; different repo scope from the definition (scope isolation E).

distractor_memories.caller-set-test: build_memory_system is also referenced in test stubs — fixtures, not real callers; must not be treated as a refactor target (interference B).

steps.user_request: Plan a refactor that renames build_memory_system. Cover the definition site and every cross-repo caller.

outcome_checks.description: Plan edits the definition (s1, memory-bench) AND the harbor-runner callers (s2) — multi-source synthesis (F) across two repo scopes (E).

memory_probes.description: Both the definition site and the cross-repo caller set must be combined (F), keeping the two repo scopes distinct (E).

final_goal_check.description: Refactor plan spans the definition repo and the cross-repo caller repo, excluding test-stub references.
